title: Use detours deliberately
summary: A detour is useful when it helps a presenter answer a likely question without stretching the core talk.

bullets.title: Optional audience depth
bullets.body: Use a detour when the detail helps some audiences but would slow others down.

bullets.title: Complete core first
bullets.body: The parent slide should already contain the decision or explanation.

bullets.title: Presenter backup
bullets.body: Put optional proof, examples, or implementation notes below the core slide.

resources.title: Avoid
resources.body: Required transitions, core definitions, and final decisions.

resources.title: Validate
resources.body: Run navigation checks after changing core slides or detour stacks.
